features.summary: Recent updates to the Firefox browser's decoder platform have focused on improving video decoding performance and compatibility. Key fixes addressed issues with Vulkan and FFmpeg, which are technologies used for rendering graphics and processing video, ensuring that the video frames are handled correctly and reducing the risk of crashes. Furthermore, restrictions were put in place for Windows Media Foundation (WMF) hardware encoders, now only allowing H264 video encoding for specific scenarios, which helps in maintaining consistent quality during low-resolution video processing. These changes matter because they enhance the browser's ability to handle high-quality video playback and improve overall stability, creating a better experience for users watching videos online. **With these adjustments, Firefox is better equipped to deliver smoother and more reliable video playback without unexpected interruptions.**

features.summary: Recent enhancements in the testing framework for Firefox have addressed critical stability issues, particularly in the area of media shutdown tests. A **fix for shutdown crashes** ensures that the browser can safely close media components without failures, while changes to serialization between MFShutdown (Media Foundation shutdown process) and MFTEnumEx (used for enumerating media transforms) help to prevent deadlocks that could freeze the system during these operations. Additionally, a new GTest has been implemented to verify a specific bug fix, further strengthening the robustness of media handling in Firefox. These updates are vital for maintaining a smooth user experience in multimedia functionalities and ensuring that developers can rely on stable testing processes.

features.summary: Recent updates to the autoplay feature in Firefox have improved how the browser handles user interactions. By removing the restriction on shortcut keys for user activation, users can now enjoy smoother autoplay experiences without needing to rely solely on mouse clicks. Additionally, touch input will now generate user activation as long as it isn't interrupted, making it easier for mobile users to engage with media content. These changes are significant because they enhance accessibility and user experience when interacting with video and audio on websites, allowing for more seamless playback. **This ultimately means users can expect a more responsive and intuitive interaction with media.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the Gecko Media Plugins (GMP) enhance the browser's ability to handle media formats and improve user experience during streaming. These changes focus primarily on optimizing **support for encrypted media**, which is essential for accessing content protected by Digital Rights Management (DRM). This improvement not only ensures smoother playback of various video formats but also broadens the range of services users can enjoy, such as movie streaming and online concerts. Overall, these adjustments aim to make Firefox a more robust platform for media consumption, ensuring users have access to the latest audiovisual content without interruptions.

features.summary: Recent changes in the Media IPC (Inter-Process Communication) area of Firefox enhance how the browser handles digital rights management (DRM) and video encoding technologies. By adjusting the lifetime management of the Content Decryption Module (CDM), which is crucial for securely playing DRM-protected content, Firefox improves the efficiency of video playback. Additionally, filtering hardware and software support in the video encoding process based on user preferences ensures that the browser can optimize performance, whether users choose to rely on hardware acceleration or software, thus making video streaming smoother and more adaptable to different system capabilities. These updates are part of a broader effort to enhance user experience and maintain robust DRM support.

The addition of a CMAF (Common Media Application Format) test without SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) enhances the ability to evaluate how well the browser handles HEVC encoded content. These improvements ensure that Firefox can better support modern video formats, which is essential for delivering high-quality streaming experiences on the web. **This is significant as it directly impacts how efficiently users can enjoy high-definition video content online.**

features.summary: Recent updates to the media sink component of Firefox focus on improving the handling of audio playback. The integration of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** will enhance the logging capabilities within the media sink, making it easier for developers to diagnose issues related to audio streaming and playback. Additionally, by removing dependence on **TaskQueue::IsEmpty()** during the initialization of the *AudioSinkWrapper*, the process will become more efficient and reliable. These changes aim to improve audio performance and stability, ultimately providing a better listening experience for users.

features.label: Utils

patches.author: Florian Quèze
patches.subject: Remove incorrect HasVideo() assertion when reporting the video resize profiler marker,

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 9: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in utils, mediacapabilities and imagecapture.

features.summary: Recent updates in the Utils area of Firefox focused on improving internal logging and video processing. One important change was the removal of an incorrect assertion related to the HasVideo() function when reporting the video resize profiler marker, which helps developers accurately track video performance metrics. Additionally, parts of the system have been updated to utilize **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a standardized logging format, improving consistency across video capabilities and image capture functions. These changes are significant as they enhance the developer experience and contribute to more reliable video handling in the browser.

features.label: WebVTT / captions

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 6: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webvtt.

patches.author: Emilio Cobos Álvarez
patches.subject: Fix HTMLTrackElement::LoadResource when load gets clobbered.

features.summary: Recent changes to the WebVTT (Web Video Text Tracks) system in Firefox enhance how captions and subtitles are handled. By incorporating **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, developers can now better track and debug issues related to caption rendering, which can improve the viewer's experience by ensuring that subtitles appear more reliably during video playback. Additionally, a fix to the `HTMLTrackElement::LoadResource` addresses a potential issue where loading captions could get interrupted unexpectedly, leading to a smoother and more consistent display of text tracks in videos. These improvements are significant for anyone relying on accurate and timely subtitles in their video content.

features.label: Driftcontrol

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 7: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in driftcontrol.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Driftcontrol area in Firefox introduced the use of **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a logging format that enhances the browser’s ability to track and analyze the performance of its features. This change primarily aims to improve debugging and maintenance, ensuring that developers can more easily identify and fix issues related to drift control, which is essential for synchronizing audio and video playback. While users may not see immediate visible changes, this improvement is crucial for maintaining a smoother and more reliable media experience in Firefox.

features.label: Encrypted Media (EME / DRM)

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 16: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in eme.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) in Firefox focus on improving how logging information is captured within the Digital Rights Management (DRM) framework. The implementation of **MOZ_LOG_FMT** enhances the clarity and structure of the logging process, making it easier for developers to diagnose issues related to media playback and licensing. This change is significant because better logging helps ensure that users experience smooth playback of DRM-protected content, such as movies and music. _There is no visible change_ in user features, but it sets the groundwork for more reliable media experiences in the future.

features.label: Media diagnostics (doctor)

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 25: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in DecoderDoctor non-DDMOZ_LOG logging.

features.summary: Recent updates to the media diagnostics tool in Firefox have enhanced the way logging is handled by implementing **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a standardized formatting for logging messages. This change aims to improve the clarity and consistency of logs when diagnosing media issues, such as playback problems with formats like HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) and WebRTC (the technology behind video calls). By refining these logs, developers can more effectively troubleshoot and understand media playback failures, which ultimately leads to a better user experience _in the long run_.

features.label: Media encoders

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 15: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in encoder.

features.summary: Recent updates to the media encoders in Firefox introduced the use of **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a standardized logging format for enhancing the development and debugging experience. This change allows developers to track the performance and behavior of media encoding processes more efficiently, which is crucial for technologies like WebRTC, responsible for real-time communication. While users may not notice any visible change, this improvement is significant for maintaining and optimizing the quality of media features in Firefox moving forward.

features.label: Mp4

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 10: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in mp4.

features.summary: Recent changes in the MP4 area of the Firefox browser focused on improving the logging capabilities for handling MP4 files, which are a popular format for audio and video. By utilizing **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, a specific logging format, developers can better track and diagnose issues related to MP4 playback and functionality. This enhancement is crucial for maintaining and improving performance, ensuring a smoother user experience when streaming or playing MP4 content.

features.label: Tests

patches.author: Ryan Curran
patches.subject: Re-enable dom/media/tests/crashtests/1490700.html on macOS 15.30 aarch64

features.summary: Recent updates have focused on re-enabling specific media test cases in Firefox, particularly the crash test for media handling on macOS 15.30 for ARM-based systems (aarch64). This change is significant because it ensures that Firefox's media functionalities, which include various content formats and playback features, are thoroughly tested for stability and reliability on this platform. By addressing and enabling these tests, developers can better identify and resolve potential issues that may affect user experience, especially for those using newer Macs. **This commitment to robust testing is crucial for maintaining high standards in media playback, **ensuring smoother video and audio experiences on Firefox.

features.label: Web Speech

patches.author: Alastor Wu
patches.subject: Part 5: Use MOZ_LOG_FMT in webspeech.

features.summary: Recent updates to the Web Speech area of Firefox have introduced the use of **MOZ_LOG_FMT**, which enhances the logging capabilities related to voice recognition and speech synthesis features. This change allows developers to better track and diagnose issues within the Web Speech API, the technology that enables interactive voice-based communication on the web. Improved logging can lead to faster problem-solving, ensuring a smoother and more reliable experience for users who rely on speech technologies for accessibility or convenience. _Overall, these updates help maintain the quality and performance of voice features, though users may not notice any immediate visible change._

features.label: WebM

patches.author: Andrew Osmond
patches.subject: Ensure MatroskaDemuxer extracts audio codec binary blob for Opus.

features.summary: Recent updates to Firefox improve how the browser handles audio in WebM files, specifically by enhancing the MatroskaDemuxer, which is responsible for reading content from Matroska format files, including WebM. With this change, the Demuxer can now properly extract the audio codec data for Opus, a codec known for its efficiency and high-quality audio streaming. This improvement is significant because it helps ensure that audio playback from WebM files is clearer and more reliable, directly enhancing the user's listening experience when using web applications that leverage this format. **This means users can expect better audio quality when playing videos or music encoded in WebM, particularly when they contain Opus audio.**
